data.title: GOOGL vs. AMZN: Which 'Strong Buy' Internet Stock Is Wall Street's Favorite Ahead of Earnings?
data.description:
Tech giants Alphabet (GOOGL) and Amazon (AMZN) are set to report their quarterly earnings next week, and investors are debating which stock looks more attractive. Both carry Strong Buy ratings from Wall Street ahead of the reports. However, analysts see more upside in Amazon, with potential gains of 22%, compared with about 3% for Alphabet.

data.title: Is the Gold Rally Over? Not So Fast, Says UBS
data.description:
Gold's (XAUUSD) historic rally has led to concern for some investors, who expect the precious metal's momentum to run dry. On the other side of the argument is UBS, which raised its gold price target for March, June, and September to $6,200 from $5,000.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

UBS expects the upside to be driven by central bank demand, strong exchange-traded fund (ETF) inflows, and more purchases of physical gold. The investment firm's bull case sees gold jumping to $7,200, while its bear case estimates a price of $4,600, with both targets representing a roughly one standard deviation move.

Where Will Gold Trade By the End of 2026?

At the same time, UBS expects the metal to end 2026 at $5,900, which still implies upside of about 10% from current levels. Factors to consider include whether the Fed is hawkish or dovish and the scope of geopolitical tensions.

UBS joins a growing chorus of Wall Street firms with high expectations. Earlier this week, both Deutsche Bank and Societe Generale raised their year-end price targets to $6,000.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Analysts Lower Microsoft (MSFT) Stock Price Target After Q2 Print, But Stay Bullish
data.description:
Microsoft (MSFT) stock plunged 10% on Thursday as the tech giant's elevated capital expenditure sparked concerns among investors. Moreover, investors were unimpressed with the slowdown in Azure's growth. Notably, revenue from Azure and other cloud services grew 39% in Q2 FY26, reflecting a slowdown compared to the 40% growth in the fiscal first quarter. Several analysts lowered their price targets following the Q2 FY26 earnings release to reflect the near-term challenges. Nevertheless, they remain bullish about Microsoft's long-term growth potential.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Analysts React to Microsoft's Q2 Print

Following the Q2 results, Goldman Sachs analyst Gabriela Borges lowered the price target for Microsoft stock to $600 from $655 and reiterated a Buy rating. The 4-star analyst thinks that the post-earnings decline in the stock reflected investors' reaction to another consecutive quarter of higher-than-anticipated capital spending without a "commensurate" rise in Azure growth rate. Nonetheless, Borges remains bullish as she believes that Microsoft's prioritization of compute capex for first-party applications (Copilot) and internal research and development (for instance, on Microsoft AI) over near-term Azure growth will enhance its AI positioning and support stronger medium-term returns.

Likewise, Piper Sandler analyst Hannah Rudoff slashed his price target for Microsoft stock to $600 from $650 and reaffirmed a Buy rating. Rudoff noted that Azure's Q2 FY26 revenue growth of 38% (at constant currency) compared to 39% in Q1 FY26 and a "steady" guidance in the range of 37% to 38% for the fiscal third quarter once again lagged investor expectations. Rudoff argued that while investors remain fixated on Azure and capex-plus-lease growth, management underscored its commitment to the broader platform, with capacity allocated across Azure, first-party apps, and internal R&D.

Rudoff remains bullish on MSFT stock as demand continues to outstrip supply. The analyst believes that Azure's growth rate mainly reflects allocated capacity and not end-customer demand. He also noted that MSFT's total capital spending is shifting toward short-lived assets (GPUs/ CPUs) to address immediate capacity constraints. Rudoff's bullish thesis is also based on MSFT's commercial RPO growth (up 110% to $625 billion, including OpenAI). "MSFT remains one of the best-positioned platforms to capitalize on incremental AI workloads," concluded the analyst.

Additionally, KeyBanc analyst Jackson Ader lowered his price target for Microsoft stock to $600 from $630 and retained a Buy rating. "Azure constant currency growth was a disappointment relative to expectations and next quarter's guide was even less inspiring, said the analyst. Ader explained that MSFT lowered his price target as concerns shift from capacity to allocation. He added that for several quarters, capacity has been viewed as the main constraint on Azure's revenue growth, but it is now clear that allocation is also an issue. Ader noted that Microsoft is prioritizing first-party AI products like Copilot and internal research more than his expectations when distributing GPUs, creating a trade-off between short-term results and long-term benefits.

Is Microsoft Stock a Buy, Hold, or Sell?

Wall Street remains bullish on Microsoft stock, with a Strong Buy consensus rating based on 35 Buys and one Hold recommendation. The average MSFT stock price target of $604.60 indicates 39.5% upside potential.
data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.title: Tesla Stock Climbs on Reported SpaceX Merger Talks — What It Means for Investors
data.description:
Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) shares are up nearly 3% as of this writing, climbing after reports suggested that SpaceX is exploring a potential merger with Tesla or, alternatively, with artificial intelligence firm xAI. The discussions are said to be preliminary, with no final decisions made, but the market is already weighing what such a move could mean for investors.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

From an investor's perspective, the appeal is rooted in how these businesses could reinforce each other rather than in any short-term deal mechanics. Musk has discussed plans for SpaceX to support advanced AI computing through space-based data centers, while Tesla already plays a key role in energy storage and large-scale battery systems. A combination could align SpaceX's future data center ambitions with Tesla's manufacturing and power capabilities, creating an integrated ecosystem that spans hardware, energy, and AI.

There is also a valuation angle that investors cannot ignore. Tesla's market value is currently around $1.4 trillion, while SpaceX is reportedly targeting an IPO valuation near $1.5 trillion. Any form of consolidation between two companies of that scale would be one of the largest corporate tie-ups ever discussed, which naturally draws interest from major funds and sovereign investors who prefer assets with long-term infrastructure characteristics. Reports suggest that such a deal might require substantial financing, indicating that outside capital would likely be involved, rather than Tesla simply absorbing SpaceX on its own balance sheet.

For Tesla shareholders, the main question is whether this would dilute focus or enhance growth. Supporters of the idea argue that Tesla would gain exposure to SpaceX's future revenue streams, particularly if space-based computing and communications become meaningful markets. At the same time, Tesla's existing expertise in batteries and energy systems could become more valuable if they are used beyond Earth-bound applications, broadening the company's addressable market well beyond cars and robotics.

There is also the strategic timing to consider. SpaceX is reportedly weighing a public listing as early as June and could seek to raise as much as $50 billion, which would make it the largest IPO on record. If merger talks advance ahead of that process, Tesla investors could end up indirectly owning a stake in a business that many see as one of the most valuable private companies in the world.

For now, no deal has been agreed, and the companies could remain separate. However, the fact that such combinations are being discussed highlights how Elon Musk is thinking about unifying his ventures around AI, energy, and infrastructure. It reframes Tesla not just as an automaker or robotics play, but as a core piece of a much larger technology platform with potential upside tied to space, data, and computing over the long term.

Is Tesla stock a Buy?

Wall Street's view on Tesla remains cautious rather than enthusiastic. Based on 28 analyst ratings over the past three months, the stock carries a Hold (i.e., Neutral) consensus, with 11 Buys, 11 Holds, and 6 Sells. The average 12-month price target stands at $401.27, which implies ~4% downside from the current share price, suggesting analysts see limited upside at present levels. (See TSLA stock analysis)
data.author: Ben Yoffe

data.title: Netflix (NFLX) Valuation Reset Spurs Stock Bulls as WBD Deal Casts Shadow
data.description:

That's why even Netflix's (NFLX) strong fourth-quarter earnings—marked by solid operating performance and confident growth guidance—failed to fully calm investor nerves amid lingering questions about the expected returns from its acquisition of Warner Bros. data.title: META Stock Cheaper than GOOGL by 'Historical Norms,' Jefferies Says after Q4
data.description:
Meta Platforms (META) stock is up over 7% after it delivered a solid Q4 beat. Attention is turning to whether META's current valuation leaves room for upside. Jefferies weighed in after the report, with five-star analyst Brent Thill noting that META trades at roughly 21x forward earnings, compared with more than 28x for Alphabet (GOOGL). He stated that the gap is much wider than "historical norms," suggesting the stock is priced at an unusually large discount.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

For context, Meta's earnings came in at $8.88 per share on $59.89 billion in revenue, beating Wall Street's expectations of $8.23 per share and $58.59 billion.

Jefferies Shrugs off Meta's High Capex    

Meta expects capital expenditures of up to $135 billion, nearly double last year's level, underscoring how aggressively the company plans to invest in AI. Wall Street estimates suggest that kind of jump in capex could cut operating margins by almost 5% this year.

Even so, Thill argues that the worst of the margin pressure is already reflected in META's stock price after its recent pullback. Meanwhile, even as Meta plans massive capital spending, it has scaled back spending on its metaverse division. Thill called that move a "positive sign," saying it shows the company is keeping a closer eye on costs.

Additionally, Thill said Meta's investments—especially in AI talent—are starting to pay off. To be precise, he noted that new text and image AI models expected in the first half of 2026 could shift the narrative around Meta's AI strategy, particularly after the Llama 4 model failed to impress.

Thill Is Bullish on META Stock

Thill reiterated a Buy rating on META stock with a $910 price target, implying more than 35% upside from current levels. He added that much of the near-term pressure on the business appears to be already priced into the stock.

Overall, Thill said Meta's revenue momentum and ongoing efficiency improvements should help offset higher operating costs, making the stock attractive to own into 2026.

What Is the Target Price for META?

On TipRanks, META stock has a consensus Strong Buy rating among 43 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 38 Buys and five Holds assigned in the last three months. The average META price target of $830.05, which implies 24% upside to the current levels.
data.author: Kirti Tak

data.title: Here's Why RBC Capital Remains Bearish on Palantir Stock (PLTR) Ahead of Q4 Earnings
data.description:
Artificial intelligence (AI)-powered data analytics company Palantir Technologies (PLTR) is scheduled to announce its results for the fourth quarter of 2025 on February 2. Ahead of Q4 2025 earnings, RBC Capital analyst Rishi Jaluria reiterated a Sell rating on PLTR stock with a price target of $50 and flagged certain concerns, including doubts about the durability of the momentum in the company's Commercial business.

Palantir Technologies stock has declined 18% over the past month amid concerns about elevated valuations of AI plays and macro headwinds. However, shares are still up more than 90% over the past year. The company has been impressing investors with stellar growth across its Commercial and Government businesses, driven by rising adoption of its Artificial Intelligence Platform (AIP).

Wall Street expects PLTR to report earnings per share (EPS) of $0.23 for Q4 2025, reflecting a 64.3% year-over-year growth. Furthermore, revenue is expected to increase by 62% to $1.34 billion.

data.title: META or MSFT: Which Is the Better Tech Stock Post-Earnings?
data.description:
Tech giants Meta Platforms (META) and Microsoft (MSFT) both reported December quarter results on January 28. Meta impressed investors with a stronger-than-expected sales outlook for Q1FY26, while Microsoft's slower cloud growth and softer margin guidance disappointed. Subsequently, META stock is rallying in pre-market trading and MSFT shares are dropping. Despite the different investor perspectives, Wall Street remains bullish on the long-term prospects of both companies, assigning a "Strong Buy" consensus.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Meta's Blockbuster Results

Meta Platforms, owner of Facebook and Instagram, beat analysts' Q4FY25 expectations with earnings per share (EPS) of $8.88, surpassing the $8.23 consensus estimate. Revenue jumped 24% year-over-year to $59.89 billion, exceeding the $58.59 billion forecast.

Additionally, Meta's daily active people (DAP) on its social media apps totaled 3.58 billion, meeting estimates. Meta forecast 2026 total expenses between $162 billion and $169 billion, with a big chunk allocated to its AI initiatives and infrastructure expansion.

Microsoft's Azure Growth Disappoints

Microsoft's adjusted EPS hit $4.14, surpassing the $3.91 consensus estimate. Revenue reached $81.27 billion, up 17% year-over-year and topping the $80.31 billion forecast. However, its Intelligent Cloud division, including Azure, reported sales of $32.9 billion, modestly beating the consensus of $32.2 billion, while analysts had expected a higher beat.

Microsoft is grappling with AI capacity constraints as demand surges faster than supply, capping near-term revenue potential. To tackle this, CFO Amy Hood highlighted last year's $88.2 billion in capital spending and announced plans for a substantial increase in fiscal 2026.

META Wins Analyst Price Target Boosts

Wells Fargo analyst Ken Gawrelski lifted his META price target from $754 to $849, implying nearly 27% upside potential. He noted that the upbeat revenue guidance, with notable Q1 acceleration, offsets elevated OpEx and CapEx projections. Earnings estimates for 2026/2027 remain steady, though stronger growth should support an expanded multiple.

Moreover, Morgan Stanley analyst Brian Nowak raised his price target to $825 (from $750), suggesting 23.4% upside potential. He boosted Meta's FY27 EPS estimate by 10%, citing Meta's investments in engagement and monetization. Nowak highlighted that these efforts are fueling the fastest growth in over four years, when the business was roughly half its current size.

Also, Jefferies analyst Brent Thill boosted his price target from $910 to $1,000, implying 49.5% upside. He noted that Meta delivered strong revenue upside, with AI-driven growth reacceleration validating returns on its massive investment cycle. Thill added that Meta provided several data points showing AI powering its core business flywheel.

Analysts Trim MSFT Price Targets

Wells Fargo analyst Michael Turrin cut his price target from $630 to $615, implying 27.7% upside potential. Despite Azure capacity constraint concerns, Turrin views the Q3 guidance as signaling improved growth potential in the second half of 2026 and beyond with added capacity.

Similarly, Citi analyst Tyler Radke lowered his price target to $635 (from $660), implying 31.8% upside. He stated that Azure showed slight upside but growth slowed marginally due to capacity allocation prioritizing first-party services. While Radke trimmed Azure estimates post-earnings, he highlighted accelerating momentum for Copilot.

Meanwhile, Morgan Stanley analyst Keith Weiss maintained his $650 price target but removed Microsoft stock from his "Top Pick" list. He noted that investor focus zeroed in on GenAI indicators like Azure growth (38% cc, just 1 point above guidance) and M365 Commercial Cloud, missing hopes for a 2%-3% beat.

Which Is Better: Meta or Microsoft?

Based on TipRanks Stock Comparison Tool, both Meta and Microsoft have a Strong Buy rating, with META shares offering a modestly higher upside potential over the next twelve months. META also has a higher TipRanks Smart Score of eight, which implies it can outperform market expectations.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: AAPL Earnings: Apple Stock Rises after Smashing Estimates
data.description:
Shares of Apple (AAPL) rose in after-hours trading after the tech giant reported earnings for its first quarter of Fiscal Year 2026. Earnings per share came in at $2.84, which beat analysts' consensus estimate of $2.68 per share. Sales increased by 15.7% year-over-year, with revenue hitting $143.76 billion. This also beat analysts' expectations of $138.39 billion.

data.title: Stock Market Today: SPY, QQQ Lose Steam on Tech Weakness as Trump Prepares Fed Chair Pick
data.description:
Both the S&P 500 ETF (SPY) and the Nasdaq 100 ETF (QQQ) closed lower on Thursday as tech and software stocks sold off sharply.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Microsoft (MSFT) closed with a 9.9% loss despite reporting higher-than-expected revenue and adjusted earnings per share (EPS) for the period ended December 31. Microsoft joined a broad selloff in software stocks, with ServiceNow (NOW), Salesforce (CRM), and Workday (WDAY) also posting steep drawdowns. Investors remain concerned about the disruptive effects of AI that could potentially intensify competition in the software space and lower margins.

During a Cabinet meeting, President Trump said that he would announce his nominee for the Fed Chair position "sometime next week." Trump ramped up criticism of Fed Chair Jerome Powell after the central bank voted 10-2 on Wednesday to hold rates steady between 3.50% and 3.75%. Powell's term is set to end on May 15, although his term as a Governor will last until January 31, 2028.

"He is hurting our Country, and its National Security," Trump said in a Truth Social post on Thursday. "We should have a substantially lower rate now that even this moron admits inflation is no longer a problem or threat."

BlackRock's (BLK) Rick Rieder is the front-runner to be nominated by Trump to lead the Fed, according to prediction platform Polymarket. Rieder holds 38% odds, followed by former Fed Governor Kevin Warsh at 30% and current Fed Governor Christopher Waller at 14%.

Before the market open, the Department of Labor (DOL) announced that initial jobless claims fell by 1,000 to 209,000, above the consensus estimate of 205,000. In addition, the DOL revised the prior week's claims to 210,000 from 200,000. Initial jobless claims are an indicator of layoffs and track how many people have applied for unemployment benefits for the first time. The data comes after Amazon (AMZN) announced it would lay off 16,000 employees, bringing its total since October to 30,000.

Continuing jobless claims, which trail initial claims by one week and track the number of people continuing to receive unemployment benefits, fell by 38,000 to 1.827 million and were below the estimate of 1.850 million. However, the DOL revised the prior week's figure to 1.865 million from 1.849 million.

Elsewhere, the U.S. trade deficit for November nearly doubled to $56.8 billion from $29.2 billion in October, driven by a 5% rise in imports to $348.9 billion. Rising imports are a headwind to gross domestic product (GDP), as they reduce net exports.

The S&P 500 (SPX) closed with a 0.13% loss, while the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) fell by 0.53%.

data.author: Eddie Pan
